name: Local Authentication (Face ID / Touch ID)
promptInjection:
CAPABILITY: Local Authentication (Face ID / Touch ID)

USAGE:
- import LocalAuthentication
- Create a new LAContext() for EACH authentication attempt (do not reuse across calls).
- Check canEvaluatePolicy(.deviceOwnerAuthenticationWithBiometrics, error: &error) to see if biometrics are available.
- Call evaluatePolicy(.deviceOwnerAuthenticationWithBiometrics, localizedReason: "Unlock your data") { success, error in ... }
- The localizedReason string appears in the Face ID dialog — make it clear and user-friendly.
- Dispatch UI updates to main thread inside the completion handler.

FALLBACK:
- Use .deviceOwnerAuthentication (without "WithBiometrics") to allow device passcode as fallback.
- Also provide an in-app manual PIN/password option for accessibility.

PERMISSION:
- NSFaceIDUsageDescription is required in Info.plist for Face ID (build system auto-detects).
- Touch ID does not require a usage description.

SIMULATOR SUPPORT:
- Face ID works in Simulator via Features > Face ID > Enrolled.
- Provide a bypass/demo mode so the app is testable without Face ID enrollment.

ERROR HANDLING:
- Handle LAError.userCancel — user dismissed the dialog.
- Handle LAError.userFallback — user tapped "Enter Password".
- Handle LAError.biometryNotAvailable — device doesn't support biometrics.
- Handle LAError.biometryNotEnrolled — no fingerprint/face registered.

ANTI-PATTERNS:
- Do NOT reuse an LAContext across multiple evaluatePolicy calls — create a fresh one each time.
- Do NOT block the main thread waiting for evaluatePolicy — it is async.
- Do NOT forget to handle all LAError cases gracefully.
- Do NOT hide the "Go to Settings" path when biometrics are not enrolled.
antiPatterns: Reusing LAContext across multiple evaluatePolicy calls (create a fresh one each time)
Blocking the main thread with evaluatePolicy
Not providing a passcode fallback option
